info.title: OntoloSQL Designer Desktop API
info.description:
Versioned API for native desktop clients. Web SPA authentication remains Sanctum cookie/session based; this API uses Google OAuth with PKCE and expiring Sanctum bearer tokens.

Version 1.1.0 documents ontology value types in detail, including enum value types (a string base type with a `oneOf` constraint), and the visual-only schema markers used for reference tables, reference links, pipeline transforms, and table-level Foundry edit settings (`editsEnabled` / `editsHistory`).

tags.name: Foundry
tags.description: Read-only Foundry Platform access scoped per ontology diagram, using delegated per-user OAuth. Diagram owners may set any host; connecting/querying requires an admin-configured host (hybrid model).

get.description: Stores the PKCE request and redirects the system browser to Google OAuth.
Validates the Google account domain, creates a one-time desktop grant, and redirects to the configured native callback URI with code and state.

302.description: Redirect to native desktop callback URI

post.summary: Exchange one-time OAuth grant for bearer token

get.description: Requires abilities: desktop.

get.description: Public endpoint hit by the browser following Foundry's redirect. Identified by the one-time server-side state. Redirects back to the diagram (web) or the desktop redirect URI.

302.description: Redirect back to the app or desktop client with a foundry status query parameter.

get.description: Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:read plus diagram read permission. Ontology diagrams only.

put.description: Requires abilities: desktop, diagrams:write and diagram ownership. Ontology diagrams only. Owners may enter any valid HTTPS host.

get.summary: Get my Foundry connection status for this diagram
get.description: Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:read plus diagram read permission.

post.summary: Begin Foundry OAuth for this diagram's host
post.description: Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:connect plus diagram read permission. Each user connects their own account. Desktop clients may pass a loopback redirect_uri.

get.description: Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:read plus diagram read permission. Uses the caller's own Foundry connection.

parameters.name: folderRid
parameters.description: Folder/project RID; falls back to the diagram default.

Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:read plus diagram read permission. Enabled Foundry AIP models available for the diagram's host (host-specific plus global).
Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:read plus diagram read permission. Sessions are shared across diagram collaborators.

post.description: Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:llm plus diagram write permission. Ontology diagrams only; the agent must be enabled.

get.summary: Get a diagram agent session with messages
get.description: Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:read plus diagram read permission.

patch.description: Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:llm plus diagram write permission.

post.description: Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:llm plus diagram write permission. Sessions are archived, never deleted.
Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:llm plus diagram write permission.

Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:llm plus diagram write permission. Sends the full diagram context and the prompt to the Foundry AIP LLM proxy using the caller's own Foundry token, and returns the assistant message with a proposed patch. The patch is reviewed and applied client-side; it is not auto-applied.

allow_destructive.description: Permit delete/rename operations in the proposed patch.

post.summary: Mark an assistant patch as applied
post.description: Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:llm plus diagram write permission. Persists that the patch was applied so reopening the agent panel won't offer to apply it again.

delete.description: Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:llm plus diagram write permission. Clears the applied flag after the change is undone so it can be applied again.

post.summary: Connect this diagram's host with a Foundry token
post.description: Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:connect plus diagram read permission. Stores a per-user token connection for the diagram's host. Works even when the host has no OAuth client configured. The token is verified against the host before being stored.

token.description: A Foundry personal or service token.

expires_at.description: Optional expiry; omit for non-expiring tokens.

get.description: Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:read plus diagram read permission. Top-level filesystem spaces.

get.summary: List children of a space, project, or folder
get.description: Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:read plus diagram read permission. Pass the parent rid; falls back to the diagram's default project/folder.

parameters.name: rid
parameters.description: Parent space/project/folder RID. Falls back to the diagram default.

get.description: Requires abilities: desktop, foundry:read plus diagram read permission.

FoundryConnectionId.name: connection
FoundryConnectionId.description: Foundry connection id owned by the authenticated user.

Forbidden.description: Authenticated token lacks the required ability or diagram permission

Unauthorized.description: Missing, invalid, or expired bearer token

FoundryConflict.description: Foundry connection or host setup is required (e.g. foundry_connection_required, foundry_connection_expired, foundry_host_not_configured, foundry_host_not_set).

FoundryUpstream.description: Foundry upstream failure or rate limit.

DiagramSchema.description:
Vue Flow schema elements (a flat list of nodes and edges). Nodes have a `type` of `table`, `row`, or `pipeline-transform`; edges have `source`/`target` referencing node ids.

Notable `data` fields: table nodes may carry `ontologyActions`, `editsEnabled` (boolean; Foundry object patches enabled) and `editsHistory` (`{ enabled, storeAllPreviousProperties }`), `titlePropertyRowId`, and `implementsInterfaces`. Row nodes carry `sqlType`, `keyMod`, `nullable`, `indexed`, `userEdits`, and `valueTypeId` (references a ValueType `id`).

Visual-only / reference elements are marked and excluded from SQL and Maker exports but preserved in backup JSON: reference tables (`data.tableKind = 'reference'`, `data.reference = true`), reference links (`data.linkKind = 'reference'`), pipeline transform nodes (`type = 'pipeline-transform'`), and transform edges (`type = 'transform'`, `data.linkKind = 'transform'`). Any element with `data.exportable = false` is visual-only.

Runtime-only keys such as hidden, selected, dimensions, and editing are not persisted and are stripped from save/load paths.

ValueType.description: Reusable ontology value type. Only available on `ontology` diagrams. Enum value types are modeled as a `string` base type carrying a single `oneOf` constraint.

id.description: Stable client identifier. Row schema items reference it via `data.valueTypeId`.

constraints.description: Validation constraints. Only string base types may define constraints.

ValueTypeConstraint.description: A value type validation constraint. Each constraint type may appear at most once per value type. `oneOf` represents an enum (a fixed set of allowed string values).

values.description: Allowed values for the `oneOf` (enum) constraint. Must contain at least one non-empty value.

usePartialMatch.description: `regex`: allow a partial match instead of a full-string match.

FoundryLlmModel.description: An admin-allowlisted Foundry AIP model the diagram agent may call.

DiagramAgentPatch.description: Allowlisted, additive-by-default operations the agent proposes. Applied client-side after review.

prompt.description: Present on user turns when prompt storage is enabled.

applied.description: Whether this assistant patch has been applied to the diagram.

DiagramAgentSession.description: A diagram agent conversation, visible to all diagram collaborators. Archived rather than deleted.

FoundryConnection.description: A user's delegated OAuth connection to a Foundry host. Tokens are never exposed.

DiagramFoundryConfig.description: Per-diagram Foundry target. Only available on ontology diagrams; host changes are owner-only.

authorize_url.description: Open this URL to authorize with Foundry.

FoundryResource.description: A filesystem resource (project, folder, or dataset).

type.description: Resource type, e.g. dataset, folder, project.
